2604.13759.title: The cognitive companion: a lightweight parallel monitoring architecture for detecting and recovering from reasoning degradation in LLM agents
2604.13759.paper_summary: Large language model (LLM) agents on multi-step tasks suffer reasoning degradation, looping, drift, stuck states, at rates up to 30% on hard tasks. Current solutions include hard step limits (abrupt) or LLM-as-judge monitoring (10-15% overhead per step). This paper introduces the Cognitive Companion, a parallel monitoring architecture with two implementations: an LLM-based Companion and a novel zero-overhead Probe-based Companion. We report a three-batch feasibility study centered on Gemma 4 E4B, with an additional exploratory small-model analysis on Qwen 2.5 1.5B and Llama 3.2 1B. In our experiments, the LLM-based Companion reduced repetition on loop-prone tasks by 52-62% with approximately 11% overhead. The Probe-based Companion, trained on hidden states from layer 28, showed a mean effect size of +0.471 at zero measured inference overhead; its strongest probe result achieved cross-validated AUROC 0.840 on a small proxy-labeled dataset. A key empirical finding is that companion benefit appears task-type dependent: companions are most helpful on loop-prone and open-ended tasks, while effects are neutral or negative on more structured tasks. Our small-model experiments also suggest a possible scale boundary: companions did not improve the measured quality proxy on 1B-1.5B models, even when interventions fired. Overall, the paper should be read as a feasibility study rather than a definitive validation. The results provide encouraging evidence that sub-token monitoring may be useful, identify task-type sensitivity as a practical design constraint, and motivate selective companion activation as a promising direction for future work.
2604.13759.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLM）代理在多步驟任務中遭遇推理退化、循環、漂移、卡住狀態，這些情況在困難任務中發生的比例高達30%。目前的解決方案包括硬性步驟限制（突發性）或LLM作為裁判的監控（每步10-15%的開銷）。本文介紹了認知夥伴，一種具有兩個實現的平行監控架構：基於LLM的夥伴和一種新穎的零開銷探針基夥伴。我們報告了一項以Gemma 4 E4B為中心的三批可行性研究，並對Qwen 2.5 1.5B和Llama 3.2 1B進行了額外的探索性小模型分析。在我們的實驗中，基於LLM的夥伴將在易循環任務上的重複減少了52-62%，開銷約為11%。探針基夥伴，基於第28層的隱藏狀態進行訓練，顯示出+0.471的平均效應大小，且在測量推理開銷為零的情況下；其最強的探針結果在一個小的代理標記數據集上達到了交叉驗證AUROC 0.840。一個關鍵的實證發現是，夥伴的效益似乎依賴於任務類型：在易循環和開放式任務上，夥伴最為有用，而在更結構化的任務上效果則是中性或負面的。我們的小模型實驗也暗示了一個可能的規模邊界：即使在干預發生時，夥伴也未能改善1B-1.5B模型上測量的質量代理。總體而言，本文應被視為一項可行性研究，而非明確的驗證。結果提供了令人鼓舞的證據，表明子標記監控可能是有用的，確定任務類型敏感性作為一個實際的設計約束，並激勵選擇性夥伴啟動作為未來工作的有前景方向。
2604.13759.author: Rafflesia Khan et.al.
